data.title: Petco skyrockets after guidance surprises to the upside
data.description:
Petco Health and Wellness Company (WOOF) shot up 35% in late Thursday morning trading after investors latched on to the retailer's adjusted EBITDA guidance for Q1 that came in ahead of expectations. The company sees Q1 adjusted EBITDA of $92M to $94M (midpoint $93M) vs. $90.3M consensus.

Despite the big rally, Jefferies upgraded Petco (WOOF) to a Buy rating and set a price target of $5.00 (+50% upside from the current price). "Petco enters F26 headed towards growth with liquidity and profitability concerns now in the rearview mirror. The breadth of initiatives planned do not rely on an improving macro and play to the retailer's strengths, giving us confidence they can work," updated analyst Kaumil Gajrawala. The firm thinks the market is still undervaluing the progress made so far by Petco (WOOF) and the self-help improvements to come.

RBC Capital Markets also kept a bullish stance on Petco (WOOF) following the update. "It's still very much a show-me story, but mgmt is building an execution track record, which seems to have investors in the early stages of warming up to it," highlighted analyst Steven Shemesh. RBC has an Outperform rating on WOOF and a price target of $4.00.

data.title: PIMCO flags four economic risks if Iran conflict disrupts the global energy supply
data.description:
Escalating tensions between Iran and the United States are raising concerns among investors that a prolonged conflict could evolve into a broader energy supply shock with meaningful consequences for the global economy, according to a recent investment note from PIMCO.

The asset manager warned that risks would intensify if the conflict results in significant damage to energy infrastructure, extended production outages, or sustained disruptions to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical artery for global oil trade. Any prolonged constraints on energy flows could amplify volatility across commodity markets and place renewed pressure on inflation worldwide.

So far, financial markets have largely focused on the inflationary implications of rising energy prices and the potential response from central banks. Higher oil and food costs could erode real household incomes, potentially slowing consumption and economic growth. 

PIMCO also highlighted several broader transmission channels through which the conflict could impact the global economy. These include disrupted supply chains and trade flows, tighter financial conditions as risk premiums rise, and weaker business and consumer sentiment amid heightened geopolitical uncertainty.

Given these risks, the firm said investors may want to prioritize portfolio resilience. PIMCO recommends emphasizing high-quality assets, maintaining diversification across markets and sectors, and preserving sufficient liquidity to navigate potential market volatility if geopolitical tensions continue to escalate. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Rapid Micro Biosystems outlines $37M–$41M 2026 revenue target amid record system placements and margin expansion drive
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Rapid Micro Biosystems (RPID) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Robert Spignesi announced a new multisystem order from Samsung Biologics in Q1 2026, stating this "expansion yet again highlights the impact that Growth Direct delivers to the world's leading pharmaceutical manufacturers as they seek to automate and modernize their critical quality and manufacturing workflows."

  • Spignesi reported record fourth quarter revenue of $11.3 million, a 37% year-over-year increase, exceeding the company’s most recent guidance and marking the 13th consecutive quarter of meeting or exceeding expectations. He highlighted "a record multisystem order from Amgen," with systems being deployed across multiple geographies and applications, and emphasized recurring revenue strength: "consumable strength underpins recurring revenue, which increased 15% for the full year and accounted for 53% of total revenue."

  • On the MilliporeSigma partnership, Spignesi stated, "we have completed specialist training and MilliporeSigma has established customer demo labs across Europe and Asia...We continue to work closely with the MilliporeSigma team as they expand their funnel and drive sales, which we expect will meaningfully contribute to our 2026 system placements."

  • Spignesi discussed product innovation, revealing a next-generation cloud-native software platform set for release in the second half of 2026: "this new platform will leverage that experience to deliver significant additional value through AI-driven analytics and insights across our customers' global data."

  • CFO Sean Wirtjes commented, "Fourth quarter revenue increased 37% to a record $11.3 million compared to $8.2 million in Q4 2024...Product revenue, which is comprised of systems and consumable revenue increased 78% to $9.3 million in the fourth quarter compared to $5.2 million in Q4 2024."

Outlook

  • For the full year 2026, the company is guiding to total revenue in the range of $37 million to $41 million, with 30 to 38 system placements. Gross margin for the year is expected to be approximately 20%, with performance accelerating in the second half. Product margin is projected in the high single digits to low teens and service margin above 40%. Wirtjes stated, "we expect to reach and maintain positive product gross margin in each of the remaining quarters of 2026, led by improving consumable gross margin, which we expect to turn positive in the second half of the year."

  • Q1 2026 revenue is anticipated to be at least $7.5 million, including at least 5 system placements. Service revenue is expected between $2.3 million and $2.6 million.

Financial Results

  • Total Q4 revenue was $11.3 million. Product revenue reached $9.3 million, and service revenue was $2 million. The company placed 16 Growth Direct systems in Q4, ending the year with 190 systems placed globally, of which 155 are fully validated.

  • Recurring revenue in Q4 was $4.6 million, and nonrecurring revenue was $6.7 million. Fourth quarter gross margin was negative $0.3 million or negative 3%, impacted by a $1.1 million inventory write-off. Excluding this, gross margin was positive 7%. Net loss for Q4 was $12.5 million, or $0.28 per share. Cash and investments at year-end stood at $39 million. Net cash burn was $3 million in Q4.

Q&A

  • Thomas Flaten, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about the widening gap between placed and validated systems. CFO Wirtjes replied that the gap is mainly due to timing differences in customer validation processes and expects the variance to come down as backlog is worked through, stating, "it's nothing we're concerned about."

  • Flaten inquired about the percentage of placed systems within CDMOs. Spignesi noted the segment is "quite strong" and that both CDMOs and principal manufacturers are important, with CDMOs benefiting from faster product release and advanced technology adoption.

  • Daniel Arias, Stifel: Questioned gross margin confidence and risks. Wirtjes pointed to locked-in product cost reductions and expected service margin recovery as key drivers, adding, "those cost reductions kind of kicking in full bore in the second half and us getting our service revenues back up to levels where they can generate meaningful margins."

  • Arias asked about utilization ramp for Samsung and Amgen and consumables pull-through. Wirtjes expects validations for these large orders in 2026, with recurring revenue impact likely in 2027. Pull-through is projected to see single-digit year-over-year improvement in 2026, with potential step-up in 2027.

  • Anna Snopkowski, KeyBanc: Sought details on the Samsung order and validation timelines for repeat customers. Spignesi confirmed the order is larger than previous and focused in South Korea, with ongoing collaboration discussions. Repeat customers typically move faster through the sales and validation process.

  • Brendan Smith, TD Cowen: Asked about the next-gen cloud-native platform and its integration. Spignesi described it as a "complete rewrite" with phased approach, aiming to enable cloud services and analytics, and monetize new recurring revenue streams over time. Smith also queried consumable cost reduction efforts; Wirtjes highlighted step-change cost reductions from other vendors and ongoing projects with MilliporeSigma.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was generally constructive but probing, focusing on risks around gross margin sustainability, validation timelines, and the pace of recurring revenue growth. Flaten and Arias pressed for clarity on operational execution and margin levers.

  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing locked-in cost reductions, multi-year strategic partnerships, and operational discipline. Spignesi frequently referenced track record and long-term growth drivers, while Wirtjes provided detailed breakdowns and acknowledged timing-related variability.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst questions shifted more toward validation backlog and margin trajectory. Management’s confidence appeared steady, with additional emphasis on cost controls and recurring revenue durability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is now explicitly set at $37 million to $41 million in revenue and 30 to 38 system placements, up from 2025's raised guidance of at least $33 million and at least 27 placements. The company now expects 20% gross margin for 2026, compared to mid- to high single-digit gross margin for 2025.

  • Strategic focus is increasingly on recurring revenue, cost reduction, and product innovation, with new milestones such as the Samsung Biologics expansion and the planned launch of a cloud-native platform.

  • Analysts' focus this quarter was more granular on margin improvement and validation timing, as compared to last quarter's emphasis on order momentum and geographic distribution.

  • Management's tone regarding market demand and execution risk remained consistent, but with greater detail on cost containment and upside from partnerships.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing uncertainty around the timing and scale of customer purchase decisions, especially for large multisystem orders. Wirtjes stated, "our guidance continues to account for some ongoing uncertainty around the timing and scale of customer purchase decisions."

  • Gross margin risk remains as product margins were impacted in Q4 by inventory write-offs. Execution risk for margin expansion is mitigated by contractual cost reductions.

  • Analysts highlighted concerns over the lag between system placement and validation and the ramp-up timing for recurring revenue from large orders.

Final Takeaway

Rapid Micro Biosystems enters 2026 with a record system placement pace, expanding commercial partnerships, and a clear focus on recurring revenue growth and margin expansion. The company’s guidance sets ambitious targets for both top-line and profitability, supported by operational improvements, strategic collaborations, and new product innovation. Management remains confident in the durability of industry tailwinds and its ability to deliver sustained long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: YTD performance of Adobe tied ETFs as results in focus post market
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) will be in the spotlight Thursday after the closing bell, when the software giant reports its latest quarterly results.

The stock has been under persistent pressure, underscoring a difficult year for the company and broader tech sector.

In light of this, below is a list of the top 10 ETFs with allocations towards Adobe (ADBE), ranked by their year-to-date performance.

The list is led by the Scharf ETF (KAT) with a YTD performance of 1.34%, followed by the Motley Fool Value Factor ETF (MFVL) and the KraneShares Wahed Alternative Income Index ETF (KWIN). The Madison Covered Call ETF (CVRD) rounds out the positive performers with a modest 0.13% gain.

At the lower end of the performance spectrum, the Themes Cloud Computing ETF (CLOD) and the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) have experienced notable declines, with YTD performances of -14.91% and -18.88% respectively. Momentum ratings among the covered funds range from B- for the Invesco Dynamic Buyback Achievers ETF (PKW) to D- and D for several of the underperforming funds.

Here is the list ranked by YTD performance:

  1. Scharf ETF (KAT), YTD perf: 1.34%

  2. Motley Fool Value Factor ETF (MFVL), YTD perf: 1.17%

  3. KraneShares Wahed Alternative Income Index ETF (KWIN), YTD perf: 0.86%

  4. Madison Covered Call ETF (CVRD), YTD perf: 0.13%

  5. Invesco Dynamic Buyback Achievers ETF (PKW), YTD perf: -0.75%

  6. Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET), YTD perf: -8.81%

data.title: Aptiv's Versigent spin-off will unlock value for shareholders -- UBS
data.description:
Ahead of the upcoming split of the Versigent spin-off on April 1, UBS believes this transaction will unlock value for Aptiv (APTV) shareholders with a sum-of-the-parts ((SOTP)) valuation yielding a $17B equity value for the standalone business.

This led analyst Joseph Spak to upgrade Aptiv (APTV) to Buy from Neutral and lift his price target by 9% to $97.

Last week, Aptiv's (APTV) board of directors approved the plan to spin off its Electrical Distribution Systems ((EDS)) business into an independent, publicly traded company called Versigent (VGNT). The deal would give Aptiv shareholders one ordinary share of Versigent for every three shares of Aptiv.

“We believe the upcoming Versigent spin-off should help unlock value. New APTV can benefit from secular trends in auto and growth in non-auto markets, while Versigent is a market leader that can leverage its position for strong cash generation, enabling capital returns and/or M&A,” Spak said in his note to clients.

His SOTP suggests that 7.8x 2027 EV/EBITDA is a more “reasonable” multiple (versus the current valuation of 5.7x), which, if correct, means that an investor can buy APTV and receive VGNT “for free.”

“If we conservatively assume that VGNT is worth 4.5x 2027 EV/EBITDA, then that implies the market is valuing the [the remaining company] at $56 per share, or ~5.7x 2027 EV/EBITDA vs the supplier peer group at 5.7x NTM EV/EBITDA,” a valuation that Spak believes is “too dire” given a better growth and margin profile for Aptiv (APTV).

data.title: Abivax pares gain after denying AstraZeneca speculation
data.description:
Abivax (ABVX) gained 2.5% after earlier rising as much as 18% after the company denied a report that it had granted AstraZeneca (AZN) exclusive access to information until March 23 with a view to formalizing a takeover bid.

The company denied the report from La Lettre, according to an emailed statement to Bloomberg.

Abivax (ABVX) shares have jumped more than 1,700% over the past year amid buyout rumors and promising late-stage trial data for its lead asset, obefazimod, against an inflammatory bowel disease called ulcerative colitis.

data.title: Futu trades lower as depreciation of Hong Kong stock holdings weighs on client assets
data.description:
Futu's (FUTU) stock declined even as the online brokerage reported a beat in its quarterly earnings, as depreciation of clients' Hong Kong stock holdings weighed on client assets.

Shares were 4.32% lower to $146.04 during Thursday morning trading.

The Hong Kong-based company reported Q4 non-GAAP EPADS of $3.07, which beats by $0.02, and revenue of $827.2M (+45.0% Y/Y), which beats by $19.77M.

"Net asset inflow remained robust in the fourth quarter, but depreciation of clients' Hong Kong stock holdings weighed on client assets," said CEO Leaf Hua Li.

"Total client assets were HK$1.23 trillion as of quarter end, up 65.9% year-over-year and flat quarter-over-quarter," the CEO noted.

"Total trading volume hit a record HK$3.98 trillion, up 37.8% year-over-year and 2.0% quarter-over-quarter," said Li.

"U.S. stock trading volume grew 17.1% sequentially to HK$3.04 trillion, primarily driven by heightened client interests in companies across the AI value chain," he said.

"Hong Kong stock turnover declined 31.0% quarter-over-quarter to HK$821.1B amid subdued investor interests in China technology names during the second-half drawdown," he noted.

For the quarterly revenue, brokerage commission, and handling charge income grew 34.6% year-over-year to $355.9M.

Interest income increased 50.2% to $390.3M, while other income was up 78.7% to $81.0M.

data.title: Ultragenyx posts late-stage trial win for gene therapy in urea cycle disorder
data.description:
Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (RARE) announced on Thursday that its gene therapy candidate DTX301 reached the main goal in a late-stage trial for ornithine transcarbamylase ((OTC)) deficiency, the most common type of urea cycle disorder.

Characterized by excessive ammonia levels in the blood, OTC deficiency is caused by a defect in a gene that is involved in the production of an ammonia-detoxifying liver enzyme.

Citing 36-week data from its Phase 3 Enh3ance study, Ultragenyx (RARE) stated that patients on DTX301 showed an ~18% reduction in 24-hour plasma ammonia (AUC₀₋₂₄ ) with a statistically significant and clinically meaningful effect compared to those on placebo.

Notably, eight out of nine patients who had abnormal ammonia AUC₀₋₂₄ despite dietary control and current treatments witnessed normal ammonia levels swiftly, according to Ultragenyx (RARE).

Regarding safety, the company added that one patient in the DTX301 arm discontinued the trial, compared to two in the placebo group, which included a patient death. There were five instances of hyperammonemic crises requiring hospitalization in the off-drug arm, with one death, compared to a single such event in the on-drug arm with no deaths.

The multinational trial is ongoing, involving 37 patients across 10 countries, with data on its secondary endpoint related to the impact of DTX301 on the treatment burden expected in H1 2027.

data.title: Energy secretary says U.S. not yet ready to escort oil tankers through Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
The U.S. Navy is "not ready" to escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, but "quite likely" will begin to do so by the end of the month, Secretary of Energy Chris Wright said Thursday.

"We're simply not ready," Wright told CNBC in an interview. "All of our military assets right now are focused on destroying Iran's offensive capabilities and the manufacturing industry that supplies their offensive capabilities."

Tanker traffic through the Strait remains effectively halted as shipowners fear attacks by Iran, which has triggered the largest oil supply disruption in history, the International Energy Agency said in its monthly report.

The U.S. announced plans Wednesday to release 172M barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as part of a coordinated effort with the IEA to collectively release 400M barrels from global reserves.

"This is an operation that will take weeks, not months," Wright said.

The secretary also said the 172M barrels will be swapped for 200M barrels of oil that will be back in the reserve within a year, which can be achieved because the backdated energy contracts are lower than the current month.

Crude oil futures traded sharply higher Thursday as Iran stepped up attacks on oil and transport facilities across the Middle East, and Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei said in a statement that the Strait of Hormuz must remain closed as a "tool to pressure the enemy."

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for April delivery +8.8% to $94.93/bbl and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for May delivery +8.2% to $99.51/bbl.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (XLE)
